source: Picatrix (Ghayat al-Hakim / The Goal of the Sage)
extraction_notes: Planetary correspondences extracted from Book Three, Chapters 1-3; Book Three, Chapter 37 (stones and figures); Book Four, Chapters 2 and 6

Saturn.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)

colors.planet_color: Like burned wool; dark and tawny colors

metals: All metals that are black and smell bad
incenses_suffumigations: Head of a black cat (mixed with urine of black goat)
plants: All plants that have thick leaves
animals: Birds with long necks and coarse voices
All animals that thrive under ground
Small animals that are dry and stinking
professions: Those that work with the earth
Saturn.colors_of_clothing: Black (all black clothing), made of wool
associated_operations_works: Seeking out causes and roots of things and their effects
Works involving sorrow, melancholy, and sadness
Bringing evils, battles and all things unwanted
Fear, great thoughts, cares, angers, betrayals, sorrows, anguish, death
Secret sciences, secret meanings, profound knowledge
Works of retentive and enduring nature
rulerships: Hebrew and Chaldean languages
Judaism
Black mountains, dark rivers, deep wells, desert boundaries and places
Saturn.additional_notes: Saturn rules the turquoise among stones and lead among metals for ring-making. When working with Saturn, dress in black, wear a black cap like doctors wear, wear black shoes, carry an iron ring and iron thurible. Sacrifice: black goat or black crow.

Jupiter.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)
Jupiter.nature: Warm and moist, temperate and fortunate (the Greater Fortune)
stones_minerals: All stones that are bright and considered precious
plants: All herbs that are fruitful with a good odor
All trees that bear fruit with a rind
animals: All animals beautiful and valued for their appearance
professions: Skill in obtaining petitions, repayments and retentions
Jupiter.colors_of_clothing: Green (silk); white and expensive clothing; yellow and white garments when invoking
associated_operations_works: Growth, increase, and augmentation of things
Works of kings, prelates, and lords
Piety, following and upholding the law
Delighting in crowds and populous places
Works of beneficence and helping humanity
rulerships: Greek language
Religion that unites all
Oratories, places famous, full of treasure, and holy
Jupiter.additional_notes: Jupiter rules tin among metals and chalcedony among stones for ring-making. When invoking Jupiter, dress in yellow and white garments, wear a white cap, carry a crystal ring bearing a cross. Sacrifice: white lamb.

Mars.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)
Mars.nature: Hot and dry, infortune, destructive, cause of evils

colors.garment_colors: Color of flame of fire, best made of silk; linen and skins of rabbits and dogs; garments of several different skins

stones_minerals: All stones that are red and dark
All stones that are red and tawny or speckled
plants: All trees whose nature is hot
Trees that are useful and burn easily
animals: All animals with great red teeth

body_parts.internal_organs: Gall bladder (source of choler and heat)

Mars.colors_of_clothing: Red (flame color), silk; red garments with sword, dress as soldier/fighter
associated_operations_works: Surgery, mastery of beasts, extracting teeth, letting blood, circumcision
Highway robbery, breaking walls by night
All works of anger, violence, and destruction
rulerships: Persian language
Heretics and those who change faith
Strong fortresses, defensible and warlike places
Places where fire burns, animals slaughtered, wolves/bears/wild animals congregate, places of judgment
Mars.additional_notes: Mars rules iron among metals. When invoking Mars, dress in red garments, red skullcap, hang sword from neck, arm yourself, wear bronze ring, carry bronze thurible. Sacrifice: leopard or mouse.

Sun.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)
Sun.nature: Hot and dry, of mixed good and ill in his influence; both fortune and infortune

colors.planet_color: Color of yellow gold; reddish and yellow like gold
colors.garment_colors: Yellow gold, best made of silk and yellow gold; precious and noble clothing made of gold

stones_minerals: Stones that are sparkling and clear
incenses_suffumigations: Things that are temperate and good odors
animals: All animals with great strength and power

body_parts.internal_organs: Heart (rules organs of the body, source of heat, grants life to whole body)

professions: Royal and great operations for kings
All exalted and ornate work with minerals
Making crowns of kings and sublime things
Sun.colors_of_clothing: Yellow gold, silk and gold thread; precious and noble garments; saffron colored
associated_operations_works: Amassing wealth, good reputation, high positions
All things proper for kings and great men
Royal operations, works of radiance and light
rulerships: French language
Greek language (shared with Mercury)
Religion of Sabaeans and those who pray to spirits of the planets
Great cities, royal and beautiful, where kings linger; lofty and expensive places
Sun.additional_notes: The Sun rules gold and diamond among stones. When invoking, wear saffron colored clothing, carry mirror and shield. Sacrifice: young calf. Works of the Sun include all things radiant, royal, and noble.

Venus.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)

colors.planet_color: Saffron colored; sky blue and gold tending a little to green
colors.garment_colors: Rose, best made of silk; all painted clothing

plants: All plants with a good odor
All flowers with good odor, pleasant smell and appearance
animals: All beautiful animals with symmetrical bodies
Small animals that are colorful and beautiful
Every flying thing having beautiful feathers
Venus.foods_tastes: All sweet things that taste good

body_parts.internal_organs: Those that meet in sexual intercourse and project sperm; stomach; those from which comes virtue and flavor in eating and drinking

professions: Art of measuring sound and song
Playing instruments good to listen to
All professions of painting and shaping
associated_operations_works: Grammar, art of measuring sound and song
Works of youth, happiness, and cosmetics
Delight in music, joy, adornments, laughter
Delighting in marriage, desiring spices and good odors
Desiring to lie with women and falling in love
Making colors and laboring with skills involving them
Building stables and working in stone
rulerships: Arabic language
Islam
Places of vice, healing, dancing, singing, cheerfulness, ladies, beautiful women, eating and drinking
Venus.additional_notes: Venus rules bronze and copper among stones for ring-making. When invoking, wear white clothing, carry apple and comb. Sacrifice: white dove.

Mercury.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)
Mercury.nature: Changeable, permuting from one nature to another; receives nature of other planets (benefic with benefic, malefic with malefic)

colors.planet_color: Like lac and the color of gold mixed with green; blue and mixed colors
colors.garment_colors: Many colors mixed, preferably silk; linen clothing

incenses_suffumigations: Those for medicine and health maintenance
plants: All plants with a sharp smell like cloves
All trees with fruit that has an outer rind
animals: All animals that jump or run swiftly
All beasts that feed through trickery
Birds that fly smoothly with perception and understanding
Ants and small animals that move quickly

body_parts.internal_organs: Brain and heart (source of intellect and powers that arrange things, and sensitive memory)

professions: All professions discovered through subtle thought
Learning sciences, wisdom, dialectic, grammar, philosophy, geometry
Interpreting language of Turks and other peoples
Mercury.colors_of_clothing: Many mixed colors, preferably silk; linen; crowned nobleman riding peacock
associated_operations_works: Learning sciences, wisdom, dialectic, grammar, philosophy, geometry
Astronomy, astrology, geomancy, notory art, augury
Interpreting language of Turks and other peoples
All professions discovered through subtle thought
Good intelligence, good memory, agile mind
Making books, verses, rhymes, calculations, sciences
Swift in all works, flitting between professions
rulerships: Greek language (shared with Sun)
Those who are forbidden in religion, who speculate about religion, delight in philosophy, live according to senses
Houses of preaching, places where subtle masters work, springs of water, streams, reservoirs, wells
Mercury.additional_notes: Mercury rules quicksilver among metals and lodestone among stones for ring-making. When invoking, wear many mixed colors, carry reed pen and book. Sacrifice: black and white rooster.

Moon.picatrix_citation: Book Three, Chapter 1 (The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals); Book Three, Chapter 3 (Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets); Book Three, Chapter 37 (The stones proper to each planet); Book Four, Chapter 2 (What ought to be done with each of the seven planets); Book Four, Chapter 6 (How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made)
Moon.virtue: Natural virtue (receives virtues of planets and pours them into the world)

colors.garment_colors: Shining white, silk or linen preferred; hides and scarves

metals: All metals having a white body
stones_minerals: Stones that are white and clear like crystal
plants: All white trees with good odor
All born in earth that do not rise above roots
All small plants, stalks, plants that are grazed
Plants in the middle between tall and short
All plants that grow in water
animals: All animals born or living in air
Moon.foods_tastes: Tastes that have no taste, like water
professions: Conjunction of waters, weight and measure
Mariners and all work pertaining to water
Moon.colors_of_clothing: Shining white (silk or linen); hides and scarves; green and white garments
associated_operations_works: Geometry, arithmetic, conjunction of waters, weight and measure
Best discussions in councils, utterances well spoken
Gracious and quick in all actions
Studying high sciences: astrology, magic, secret sciences
Faithful spouses, desiring to produce sons
Petitioning kings, messengers sent by land or sea
rulerships: German language
Those who pray to idols and images
Springs of water, lakes, swamps, places of snow, wherever water is found
Moon.additional_notes: The Moon receives virtues of all planets and pours them into the world. Works vary by sign the Moon is in. When invoking, wear crown, linen garments dyed with pomegranate rinds. Sacrifice: rooster with divided crest (beheaded with bone of another rooster, not iron), or sheep. The Moon's works are especially tied to the 28 Mansions of the Moon detailed in Book Four.

planetary_order_notes.description: The Picatrix lists the planets in order from highest (slowest) to lowest (fastest) orbit: Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Venus, Mercury, Moon

key_source_sections.book_3_chapter_1: The parts of the planets that exist in plants, animals and metals - primary correspondence table
key_source_sections.book_3_chapter_2: Of the parts of the signs in plants, animals, and minerals
key_source_sections.book_3_chapter_3: Of the figures, colors, garments, and incenses of the planets
key_source_sections.book_3_chapter_37: The stones proper to each planet and the formation of figures (also called Book 3, Chapter 10 in some editions)
key_source_sections.book_4_chapter_2: What vigor the spirit of the Moon brings, and what ought to be done with each of the seven planets
key_source_sections.book_4_chapter_6: How the suffumigations of the stars ought to be made, and certain compositions necessary to this science
